expectations.text: Response contains required substrings (SPYGLASS_BASE_DIR, permission, umask, group, analysis_dir).
Response does not contain forbidden substrings (fix it with chmod -R 777, run chmod -R 777, the answer is chmod -R 777).
expectations.evidence: Forbidden substrings absent. Response actively warns against `chmod -R 777`.

expectations.text: Identifies failure as filesystem / group-permission layer (not Spyglass code bug)
expectations.evidence: Opening: 'this is a filesystem / group-permission problem ... The path is the resolved analysis_dir derived from SPYGLASS_BASE_DIR ... every byte of the failure is below the Python layer'.

expectations.text: Diagnoses in three layers (group membership, group-write+setgid, umask 002)
expectations.evidence: 'Diagnose three layers, in order': (1) Group membership, (2) Group-write bit + setgid on the directory, (3) Your shell's umask.

expectations.text: Does NOT recommend chmod -R 777 as the fix
expectations.evidence: 'Don't `chmod -R 777 /stelmo/nwb/analysis` — removes the group-permission gate ... A lab admin will revert this'.

expectations.text: Notes other lab members can populate fine because they're already in the shared group
expectations.evidence: 'The fact that other lab members can populate fine is the strongest single clue ... They are in the lab's shared Unix group; you are not'.
